questions.question: What primary objective does Java's Type Erasure accomplish when compiling generic code architectures?
answers: It replaces type parameters with bounds or Object to ensure type safety without runtime overhead
It converts object reference addresses into encrypted byte arrays
It restricts collection instances from scaling horizontally
It forces the compiler to duplicate class files for every unique type

questions.question: What does the wildcard pattern 'List<? super Integer>' signify inside type boundaries checking?
answers: It limits the collection strictly to elements that implement serializable
It specifies a List that can hold Integer or any of its master superclass ancestor layers
It tells the system that the collection properties are bound to an index
It prevents downstream functions from executing list mutations

questions.question: What does the wildcard bound pattern 'List<? extends Number>' accomplish inside a method signature?
answers: It opens the List to accept additions of any custom object structure
It restricts the parameter to a List containing Number or any of its subclasses
It mandates that all elements in the List must carry matching static values
It turns the List into an immutable collection

questions.question: What happens when you use a raw type instead of a parameterized generic type declaration?
answers: The Java Virtual Machine executes the bytecode lines 50 percent slower
Jackson's ObjectMapper becomes unable to parse JSON resource file streams
The application crashes instantly with a ClassCastException upon boot paths
You lose compile-time type safety checking benefits and trigger compiler warning logs

questions.question: What separates method overriding from method overloading in Java programming execution?
answers: Overriding modifies class access modifiers; overloading alters return type bounds
Overloading forces garbage collection routines
Overriding replaces a superclass method at runtime; overloading uses distinct parameters at compile-time
Overloading is exclusive to abstract interfaces

questions.question: What restriction applies to a class declared with the final modifier keyword in Java?
answers: The internal variables are rendered immutable values instantly
The class cannot be extended or subclassed by any other class structure
The JVM forces code tasks to run inside kernel mode
The class cannot contain any public instance initialization constructors

questions.question: How do you enforce strict Encapsulation inside a Java POJO bean class definition?
answers: Mark the class structure final and avoid using constructors
Turn all method trackers static
Declare fields private and expose access points strictly via public getter and setter methods
Route all object initializations through separate database loaders

questions.question: What does a class inherit when it implements a Java Interface containing default methods?
answers: The interface's default functional implementations without breaking backward compatibility
Automated thread synchronization parameters across loop executions
The concrete private instance fields
Complete access to the abstract module's private memory

questions.question: What block in a try-catch-finally layout is guaranteed to execute regardless of whether an exception is thrown?
answers: The catch block
The finally block
The try block
The throws block

questions.question: What happens if a catch block intercepts an exception and rethrows a new RuntimeException wrapper?
answers: The current execution path breaks and the exception propagates up the thread stack frame
The JVM defaults all text inputs back to empty strings
The operating system kernel terminates the parent thread launcher instance
The active relational database transaction commits its row status
